atoms.semantic_claim: The Ethereum node propagates the configured maximum block tx-list size cap into the payload builder so block construction enforces `max_block_size_bytes`.

destinations.evidence: The destination retains protocol block-size enforcement but intentionally does not restore the old custom operator knob.

atoms.equivalence_argument: No equivalent destination wiring exists. The downstream node-level size cap depends on both a config trait method and a builder-config field that are absent in the v2.2.0 port.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Configure a very small `max_block_size_bytes`, then submit transactions whose combined RLP size exceeds that cap while staying under gas and blob limits; downstream should stop including them, but the destination build path has no size-cap input and would not preserve that behavior.
atoms.required_change: No code change in this v2 slice; behavior is intentionally inactive or out of scope.
atoms.notes: The old configurable builder.max-block-size surface is not active in the v2.2 slice. The active payload builder retains protocol RLP-size enforcement for Osaka blocks, but no shipped Ritual runtime path currently supplies a custom per-node block-size cap.

atoms.semantic_claim: The Ethereum node forces missing-payload resolution to race an empty payload fallback instead of awaiting the first in-progress payload, preventing consensus-client crashes/stalls when payload building fails.

destinations.evidence: The node wiring now restores the downstream missing-payload fallback by selecting RaceEmptyPayload through the existing payload-builder toggle.
The runtime behavior is preserved through the existing v2.2 payload-builder state machine.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Missing-payload resolution now races an empty payload instead of waiting for the in-progress job when no payload is available.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Induce a payload-build failure or delay so `on_missing_payload` is exercised before any payload is available; downstream should choose `RaceEmptyPayload`, while the destination path will keep `AwaitInProgress` and can reproduce the missing-payload stall/error path.
atoms.notes: Fixed in the v2.2 port during the feature-ordered pass; focused crate checks passed.

summary: Atoms in this file are intentionally absent or mapped to active v2.2 behavior where applicable.
notes: One atom is a full feature removal (`max_block_size_bytes` path absent from config and builder types); the other is an unwired existing toggle that leaves the default missing-payload behavior active.
